content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
June 14,
1995
Mr.
Mike Mount
Office of Legal Affairs
Department of Developmental Services
1600 9th Street
Sacramento,
CA 94244-2020
Re:
Your Request For Advice
Our File No.
A-95—187
Dear Mr.
Mount:
This letter responds to your request for formal written
advice regarding the mass mailing prohibitions of the Political
Reform Act
(the “Act”).
1
QUESTION
May the title
“A Governor’s Conference” appear on the cover
page of a brochure announcing a conference presented by the
Department of Developmental Services?
CONCLUS ION
Yes.
The draft brochure attached to your request for advice
does not violate he mass mailing -prohibitions of the Act.
FACTS
The Department of Developmental Services is sponsoring a
conference on the issue of infant and toddler health care.
The
conference brochure, registration materials and other announcement
mailers will contain the title
“A Governor’s Conference”
prominently displayed on the face of the material.
The Governor’s
actual name only appears in the conference itinerary as a speaker;
it is in the same typeface and size as the names of the other
speakers at the conference.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
428 .1 STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—95—187
Page
2
ANALYSIS
Government Code Section 89001 prohibits any mass mailing sent
at government expense.
The literal prohibition of Section 89001
is ameliorated by the Commission’s implementing regulation,
Regulation 18901.
Regulation 18901 states,
in part, that a mass
mailing sent at public expense is prohibited when it either
features an elected official by a photograph or prominent display
of his or her name or office,
or is sent in consultation,
cooperation,
or coordination with the elected officer and contains
the elected officer’s name, picture,
or office.
The draft
materials you submitted prominently display, the Governor’s Office
in the title.
Accordingly, unless an exception applies,
sending
the draft materials at government expense would be a prohibited
mass mailing.
Regulation 18901, subdivision(b)(9), provides that an
announcement of an official agency event, for which the agency is
providing financial support,
is exempt from the mass mailing
prohibitions provided that an elected officer’s photograph or
signature is not included and the elected officer’s name is
mentioned only once.
It would appear that the draft materials you
submitted qualify under this exemption.
The Governor’s name only
appears listed as a speaker,
and the conference is promoted by the
Department of Developmental Services.
Accordingly,
the draft
brochure and announcement that you submitted with your request
would not be a prohibited mass mailing if sent at government
expense.
If you have any further questions on this matter, please feel
free to contact the undersigned.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel 4Li&
By:
Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel,
Legal Division
SGC:DEM:ak

page of a brochure announcing a conference presented by the
Department of Developmental Services?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
The draft brochure attached to your request for advice
does not violate he mass mailing -prohibitions of the Act.
